en.extract:
The Chatham Islands snipe  (Coenocorypha pusilla), also known as the Chatham snipe,  is a species of wader in the family Scolopacidae.
It is endemic to the Chatham Islands of New Zealand, and is only found on a few islands in the south of the Chatham Islands group.
Its natural habitats are temperate forests and temperate grassland.
The Chatham Islands snipe feeds by probing into the ground in search of worms, amphipods, insects and larvae.


Scientific discovery

In 1868 the Chatham Islands snipe was collected by naturalist Charles Traill and was sent to ornithologist Walter Buller who described it as a new species of snipe. On an exploratory mission to the islands in 1871, Henry Travers only found the snipe on Mangere Island. Attempts to return snipe to main Chatham Island would be hampered by the presence of introduced mammals and of weka, which are predators of snipe chicks.


References


External links
BirdLife Species Factsheet
Chatham Island snipe discussed on RNZ Critter of the Week, 14 October 2016

The '''Chatham Islands snipe'''  ('''''Coenocorypha pusilla'''''), also known as the '''Chatham snipe''',  is a species of [[wader]] in the family [[Scolopacidae]].
It is [[endemic]] to the [[Chatham Islands]] of [[New Zealand]], and is only found on a few islands in the south of the Chatham Islands group.<ref name=TeAra />

Its natural [[habitat]]s are temperate [[forest]]s and temperate [[grassland]].

The Chatham Islands snipe feeds by probing into the ground in search of worms, amphipods, insects and larvae.<ref name=TeAra>{{cite web|title=Story: Wading birds - Chatham Island snipe|url=http://www.teara.govt.nz/en/photograph/9183/chatham-island-snipe|publisher=Te Ara - the Encyclopedia of New Zealand|access-date=11 November 2014}}</ref>

==Scientific discovery==
[[File:Chatham snipe or Chatham Island snipe (Coenocorypha pusilla) Auckland museum.jpg|thumb|Chatham Islands snipe (''Coenocorypha pusilla'') mounted skin]]
In 1868 the Chatham Islands snipe was collected by naturalist [[Charles Traill]] and was sent to ornithologist [[Walter Buller]] who described it as a new species of snipe. On an exploratory mission to the islands in 1871, [[Henry H. Travers|Henry Travers]] only found the snipe on [[Mangere Island]].<ref name=nzbirds.com>{{cite web|title=Tutukiwi, the Chatham Island snipe|url=http://www.nzbirds.com/birds/chathamsnipe.html|publisher=New Zealand Birds Ltd.|access-date=11 November 2014}}</ref> Attempts to return snipe to main Chatham Island would be hampered by the presence of introduced mammals and of [[weka]], which are predators of snipe chicks.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.radionz.co.nz/national/programmes/afternoons/audio/201819996/critter-of-the-week|title=Critter of the Week|last=Mulligan|first=Jesse|last2=Toki|first2=Nicola|date=14 October 2016|website=RNZ|access-date=21 October 2016}}</ref>
